ogTitle: Heddon Greta vs Heifer Station: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Heddon Greta and Heifer Station.
heading: Heddon Greta vs Heifer Station — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Heddon Greta, New South Wales and Heifer Station, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Heddon Greta has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Heifer Station (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Heddon Greta or Heifer Station safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Heddon Greta has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Heifer Station scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Heddon Greta scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Heifer Station scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Heddon Greta or Heifer Station?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Heddon Greta has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
